steps.subgoalLabel: Look at what needs to be matched
steps.instruction: There are 4 dogs: 🐶 🐶 🐶 🐶. Each dog needs one bone.
steps.explanation: One-to-one means each dog gets exactly one bone — not zero, not two.

steps.subgoalLabel: Match them one at a time
steps.instruction: Give the first bone to the first dog, the second bone to the second dog, and so on.
steps.explanation: Each dog is paired with exactly one bone. No dog is skipped, and no dog gets two.

steps.instruction: Count the dogs (4) and count the bones (4). They match!
steps.work: 4 dogs, 4 bones → every dog has a bone
steps.explanation: When we have the same number of each, every object gets a match. This is one-to-one correspondence.

steps.instruction: Here are some apples: 🍎 🍎 🍎 🍎 🍎. We will count them using one-to-one.
steps.explanation: When counting, each object must be matched to exactly one number.

steps.instruction: Count each apple and assign the next number. Do not skip any apple. Do not count any apple twice.
steps.explanation: Each apple gets one number. We do not say two numbers for one apple, and we do not skip an apple.

steps.instruction: The last number tells how many apples there are.
steps.work: Last number = 5, so there are 5 apples.
steps.explanation: One-to-one counting means matching each object to one number. The last number is the total.
